description_long: This chest of drawers features a rectangular, blocky form with three wide horizontal drawers on the front, each with a subtle wood grain texture. The top and sides are covered in a muted, fabric-like material with visible seams and wooden corner reinforcements, giving it a utilitarian, possibly insulated appearance. One side is finished with a grid of reddish-brown tiles, while the opposite side and back are plain, with the same fabric-like covering. A single cylindrical black pipe protrudes from the lower front, suggesting a possible utility or industrial adaptation. The drawers lack visible handles, contributing to a minimalist, functional aesthetic. The overall construction appears robust, suitable for use in garages, workshops, or service areas in urban environments.
description: A robust, three-drawer chest with wood grain fronts, fabric-like top and sides, and a tiled side panel, featuring a protruding black pipe.
description_view_0: Front view showing three wide wood-textured drawers and a central black pipe protruding from the lower section.
description_view_1: Side/top view highlighting the fabric-like covering, wooden corner reinforcements, and a tiled side panel.
description_view_2: Rear/top view showing the plain fabric-like covering and the tiled side panel, with no visible drawers or pipe.
description_view_3: Opposite side view with the black pipe extending outward and the plain wood-textured side, no tiles visible.
